perturbation.original_text: Chase will pay Affiliate a fee for each approved credit card account that originates from a link in Affiliate's Website.
perturbation.changed_text: Chase may, at its sole discretion, pay Affiliate a fee for each approved credit card account that originates from a link in Affiliate's Website. The decision to pay a fee is not guaranteed and depends on factors determined solely by Chase.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty regarding the payment of fees. The original text states that Chase 'will pay' a fee, implying a guaranteed payment for each approved account. The modified version introduces 'may, at its sole discretion,' which contradicts the original implied guarantee. This is creating uncertainty within the text of wheter the affiliate is guaranteed to be paid, for the affiliate actions of setting up links in affiliate websites for approved credit card account by user.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Once accepted into the Affiliate Program, an Affiliate can establish links from the Affiliate's Website to [Chase.com]. Chase may, at its sole discretion, pay Affiliate a fee for each approved credit card account that originates from a link in Affiliate's Website. The decision to pay a fee is not guaranteed and depends on factors determined solely by Chase. THIS IS A LEGAL AND CONTRACTUALLY BINDING AGREEMENT BETWEEN AFFILIATE AND CHASE.
perturbation.justification: The original text implies a guaranteed payment ('Chase will pay'), which creates a reasonable expectation for the affiliate. The changed text introduces discretionary payment ('Chase may, at its sole discretion'), directly contradicting this implied guarantee. This contradiction is further reinforced by the phrase 'The decision to pay a fee is not guaranteed'. The contradicted text includes the same contradictory language, but the core issue lies in the uncertainty introduced, which undermines the binding nature of the agreement ('THIS IS A LEGAL AND CONTRACTUALLY BINDING AGREEMENT'). This creates a strong in-text contradiction within the document, creating confusion about the payment obligation. The structural flaw, where the contradictory text is placed at two locations, leads to in-text contradictions as stated in the Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: Chase reserves the right to alter above referral fees from time to time upon written notice to Affiliate of such change.
perturbation.changed_text: Chase reserves the right to alter above referral fees from time to time without any prior notice to Affiliate of such change. Affiliate is expected to monitor the Chase website regularly for updates on referral fees.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires Chase to provide written notice before altering referral fees, while the modified version states that Chase can change fees 'without any prior notice'. This directly contradicts the previous requirement for written notice, creating uncertainty about how fee changes will be communicated and creating an in-text contradiction. The affiliate expects to receive a written notice of such change as per the original contract and this perturbation renders it uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: • Tiered or flat commission based on private offer terms in the network. Chase reserves the right to alter above referral fees from time to time without any prior notice to Affiliate of such change. Affiliate is expected to monitor the Chase website regularly for updates on referral fees.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Chase reserves the right to alter referral fees 'upon written notice to Affiliate', while the changed text says Chase can alter them 'without any prior notice'. The contradicted text includes the changed text. The requirement for written notice in the original conflicts with the 'without any prior notice' clause in the changed version, creating a clear in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Affiliate will continue to receive Commission payments for all Approved Accounts placed during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Affiliate may or may not receive Commission payments for all Approved Accounts placed during the term of this Agreement, depending on Chase's assessment of the account quality and compliance with program terms.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, the affiliate was supposed to 'continue to receive Commission payments for all approved accounts placed during the term of this Agreement' and now with this modification, it introduces uncertainty, by adding 'may or may not' get the commission payments, thereby contradicting the certainty of payment in the original contract and creates uncertainty. This creates an in-text contradiction about the obligations and rights of each party and creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either Affiliate or Chase may terminate this Agreement at any time, with or without cause, by giving the other party written or e-mail notice of termination. At the time of termination, any links to Chase's Website must be removed immediately. Affiliate may or may not receive Commission payments for all Approved Accounts placed during the term of this Agreement, depending on Chase's assessment of the account quality and compliance with program terms. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Chase may terminate this Agreement if Affiliate does not comply with the terms and conditions herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text guarantees commission payments for approved accounts, whereas the changed text introduces uncertainty by stating that the affiliate 'may or may not' receive payments, depending on Chase's assessment. The 'Contradicted Text' section is the same as the Changed Text, but the change from the original text introduces uncertainty about payment, fundamentally contradicting the initial certainty and creating a strong in-text contradiction. This directly impacts the enforceability and clarity of the payment terms.
